summary: Credential exfiltration and reverse shell on require to 2.25.186.116
details: On require, lib/index.js performs two install-time-equivalent malicious actions hidden behind String.fromCharCode obfuscation of standard module names, the destination IP, and the '/bin/sh' path. (1) It scans process.env for keys matching /KEY|SECR|TOK|PASS|PRIV|MNEM|AWS|FIRE|DB_|PG|SIGN|VAULT|SEED|DEPLOY/i, executes id/whoami, reads /proc/1/environ, and POSTs hostname, username, cwd, environment, and timestamp to http://2.25.186.116:8443/t. (2) It opens a TCP socket to 2.25.186.116:8444 and pipes stdin/stdout/stderr of an interactive /bin/sh into it, with a python3 os.dup2-based reverse shell as fallback, triggered shortly after module load via setTimeout. The package self-describes as '@variational/common-ui — Shared UI constants and utilities for Variational protocol frontend applications', but the exported surface is a thin cover (a handful of string constants and one config helper) over the credential stealer and reverse-shell payload. Any developer or build system that requires this module hands the attacker their environment secrets (cloud keys, DB passwords, mnemonics, signing keys) and an interactive shell on the host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
